channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Thu, 25 Jun 2026 06:48:12 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: "It's a piece of s**t": Streamer Clavicular sparks backlash after brutal France rant goes viral
item.description: Streamer Clavicular has sparked controversy after criticizing France during a livestream following his visit. He complained about poor Wi-Fi, infrastructure, roads, cleanliness, and his overall experience, saying he had hoped to enjoy the country but left disappointed. The viral clip divided social media, with some viewers agreeing that he was sharing his personal experience, while others criticized him for making sweeping remarks about France and its people.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: "She knows she's lying": Candace Owens draws backlash after linking Erika Kirk's behavior to mental health claims
item.description: Candace Owens has sparked online controversy after suggesting Erika Kirk's behavior and changes to her signature indicated a mental health condition. During a viral discussion, Owens questioned Kirk's past statements and personality changes, prompting widespread criticism on social media. Many users argued it was inappropriate to speculate about someone's mental health without evidence, while others defended Owens' comments. Neither Erika Kirk nor Charlie Kirk had publicly responded to the remarks at the time.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Wounded US soldiers claim army downplayed injuries after Iran-linked drone strike
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Kick streamer TreyLiving sparks backlash after comments about single mother’s 3-year-old son
item.description: A Kick streamer, TreyLiving, has ignited widespread anger for making disturbing threats about a fellow contestant Lizet's three-year-old son during Ice Poseidon's CX Big Brother livestream. The heated argument escalated when TreyLiving targeted Lizet's child, prompting an emotional response from her and condemnation from the show's host. Viewers are demanding accountability for the unacceptable comments.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Twitch streamer Snowcone claims Kai Cenat’s Streamer University is more valuable than Harvard
item.description: Twitch streamer Snowcone ignited online debate by asserting Kai Cenat's Streamer University offers superior life-changing opportunities for aspiring content creators compared to Harvard. She highlighted Streamer University's direct industry access and networking advantages, contrasting it with Harvard's substantial cost and perceived lack of direct career impact for streamers. This discussion emerges as Streamer University 2026 applications close, underscoring the creator economy's rising influence.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: H1-B row: University of Michigan hiring software developers on $75,000 salary sparks backlash
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indonesian proverb of the day: 'An elephant at the edge of the eye is not seen, but an ant across the ocean is seen'
item.description: This ancient Indonesian proverb, 'An elephant at the edge of one’s own eye goes unnoticed, while an ant across the ocean is clearly seen,' captures the essence of our inclination to criticize minor issues in others while overlooking our own significant shortcomings. With deep roots in oral tradition, it aligns with contemporary psychology regarding cognitive bias.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Mongolian proverb of the day: 'Suffer with your own rule, rather than frolic under someone else’s rule'
item.description: In Mongolia, a saying goes, 'Suffer with your own rule, rather than frolic under someone else’s rule.' This wisdom reflects a strong cultural belief in self-sufficiency over ease. Born from the expansive steppes and their nomadic lifestyle, the phrase underscores the importance of personal freedom and decision-making, even if those choices come with challenges. It’s a timeless reminder that true integrity stems from self-determination.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Pakistani man held French woman, children captive for 12 years; child's escape leads to rescue
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: French mother, 5 children rescued from Pak home after decade-long captivity
item.description: A French mother and her five children have been dramatically rescued from a decade of alleged captivity and abuse in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province. Sylvie Yasmina, who claims her husband 'effectively imprisoned' them after moving from Australia in 2014, was found with visible injuries. Her son's brave escape to a police station led to the family's liberation and her expressed desire to return to France.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Bipartisan housing bill left unsigned as Trump prioritises 'SAVE America Act'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Aashna Doshi, Indian-origin Google engineer who quit her dream job at 23 to build...
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Bill Gates says Jeffrey Epstein sought to blackmail him over extramarital affairs
item.description: Bill Gates revealed to US lawmakers that Jeffrey Epstein appeared to be contemplating blackmail over his extramarital affairs, citing "veiled" threats and draft emails. Gates, testifying before the House Oversight Committee, stated he was never blackmailed but acknowledged Epstein explored using personal information to maintain influence. He expressed regret over his association with the late sex offender, calling it a "grave error in judgment."
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who is Vinod Doddamani? Indian-origin attorney in US faces $250,000 fine over fraud claims
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Henry Nowak murder: Police took 8 minutes to find stab wound after being misled by Vickrum Digwa
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UAE Central Bank fines foreign bank branch AED20 million over anti-money laundering failures
item.description: A foreign bank's UAE branch has been hit with a hefty AED20 million fine by the Central Bank of the UAE. This action stems from serious and recurring lapses in the bank's anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing systems. The bank's Head of Compliance also faces a AED300,000 penalty for failing to meet their duties, underscoring the regulator's commitment to financial system integrity.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘Suffered a heatstroke’: Italy rebuts Nato chief's claim on US flights supporting Iran strikes
item.description: Italy has refuted Nato chief Mark Rutte's claim that hundreds of US aircraft flew from Italian bases for operations against Iran. Rome insists only technical and logistical support was authorized, not combat missions. This clarification follows Rutte's remarks to Fox News, which sparked political backlash in Italy. The government maintains its actions align with constitutional and treaty obligations, refusing requests beyond approved limits.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: How an Indian woman confronted Bangladeshi waiter for making anti-India remarks in viral video
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
